hadiths.chapter: 61. CHAPTER ON I'TIKAF (SPIRITUAL RETREAT)
hadiths.ar:
2104   -  وَّ رَّوَّى إِن ْن ُّ إَّلْمُّع ِي زَّة ِ عَّن ْ سَّمَّاعَّه َّ ق َّالَّ: سَّا َّلْث ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ْ مُّعْي َّكِف ٍ وَّإق غَّ إ َّهْلَّهُّ ق َّق َّال 
ن ِمَّن ْز ِلَّه ِ مَّن ْ إ َّق ْطَّزَّ ي َّوْماً مِن ْ س َّهْزِ رَّمَّص َّان
hadiths.en:
It is narrated by Muhammad ibn Sinan, from Abdul -A‘la ibn A‘yan, who said:  
"I asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} about a man who engaged in sexual relations with his wife at 
night while performing i'tikaf during the Month of Ramadan.  
Imam {a.s} said: 'He must pay one expiation.'  
I then asked: 'What if he engaged in sexual relations during the day?'   
Imam {a.s} said: 'He must pay two expiations.'"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 4, p. 292 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 11, p. 
495 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 10, p. 547 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 80, p. 134 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 61, H. 2103

hadiths.chapter: 61. CHAPTER ON I'TIKAF (SPIRITUAL RETREAT)
hadiths.ar:
2105 - وَّ رَّوَّى ذَّإوُّذُّ ن ْن ُّ إَّلْحُّصَّي ْن ِ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  إَّلْعَّن  َّاسِ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال  رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى 
إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ ق ِى  س َّهْزِ رَّمَّص َّان َّ ق ِى  إَّلْعَّس ْزِ إَّلْأ ُّولَّى ي ُّم َّ إِعْي َّكَّف َّ ق ِى  إَّلي  َّان ِ ي َّه ِ ق ِى  إَّلْعَّس ْزِ إَّلْوُّسْطَّى ي ُّم َّ إِعْي َّكَّف َّ ق ِى   
إَّلي  َّالِي َّه ِ ق ِى  إَّلْعَّس ْزِ إَّلْأ َّوَّإج ِزِ ي ُّم َّ لَّمْ ي َّز َّلْ رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ ي َّعْي َّكِف ُّ ق ِ ى  إَّلْعَّس ْزِ إَّلْأ َّوَّإج ِزِ.
hadiths.en:
It is narrated by Ibn Al-Mughira, from Sama'ah, who said:  
"I asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} about a person performing i'tikaf who engaged in sexual 
relations with their spouse.  
Imam {a.s} said: 'They are like one who has broken their fast on a day of Month of 
Ramadan.'"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 179 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 4, p. 291 
• Al-Istibsar, Part 2, p. 130 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 11, p. 494 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 10, p. 547 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 61, H. 2104

hadiths.chapter: 61. CHAPTER ON I'TIKAF (SPIRITUAL RETREAT)
hadiths.ar:
2106  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إِن ْن ُّ مَّحْن ُّوب ٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  إ َّي  ُّوب َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ن َّصِي زٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق ِى  إَّلْم عْي َّكِق َّه ِ إ ِذ َّإ طَّمِي َّث ْ  
ق َّال
hadiths.en:
It is narrated by Dawud ibn Al-Husayn, from Abu Al-Abbas, from Abu Abdullah 
{a.s}, who said:  
"The Messenger of Allah {SWT} (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) 
performed i'tikaf in the first ten days of Month of Ramadan, then in the second ten days 
(the middle ten), and then in the last ten days. After that, the Messenger of Allah {SWT} 
(peace and blessings be upon him and his family) consistently performed i'tikaf in the 
last ten days of Month of Ramadan."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 175 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 11, p. 484 • Wasa’il 
Al-Shi’a, Vol. 10, p. 534 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 16, p. 274 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 61, H. 2105

hadiths.chapter: 61. CHAPTER ON I'TIKAF (SPIRITUAL RETREAT)
hadiths.ar:
2107  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْحَّسَّن ُّ ن ْن ُّ إَّلْح َّهْمِ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ: سَّا َّلْي ُّهُّ عَّن ِ إَّلْمُّعْي َّكِف ِ ي َّا ْي ِى إ َّهْلَّهُّ ق َّال 
إِمْزَّإ َّي َّهُّ لَّي ْلاً وَّ لأَّ ن َّهَّارإً وَّ هُّوَّ مُّعْي َّكِف
hadiths.en:
It is narrated by Ibn Mahbub, from Abu Ayyub, from Abu Basir, from Abu 
Abdullah {a.s}, regarding a woman performing i'tikaf who experiences menstruation.   
Imam {a.s} said: "She should return to her home, and when she becomes pure, she 
should return and make up for what she missed."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 179 • Awaali al-La’aali, Part 3, p. 147 • 
Al-Wafi, Vol. 11, p. 493 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 10, p. 554 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 61, H. 2106

hadiths.chapter: 61. CHAPTER ON I'TIKAF (SPIRITUAL RETREAT)
hadiths.ar:
2108   -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ مَّي ْمُّون ِ ن ْن ِ مِهْزَّإن َّ ق َّالَّ: كُّن ْث ُّ ج َّالِساً عِن ْذَّ إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ ن ْن ِ عَّلِى ٍّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّا َّي َّاة رَّج ُّل  ق َّق َّالَّ لَّهُّ ي َّا  
إِن ْن َّ رَّسُّولِ إَّلل َّهِ إ ِن  َّ ق ُّلاَّن اً لَّهُّ عَّلَّى  َّ مَّال  وَّ ي ُّزِي ذُّ إ َّن ْ ي َّحْن ِسَّن ِى  ق َّق َّال هِ مَّا عِن ْذِى  مَّال  ق َّا َّق ْص ِى َّ عَّن ْك 
ق َّالَّ ق َّلَّن ِسَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ن َّعْلَّهُّ ق َّق ُّلْث ُّ لَّهُّ ي َّا إِن ْن َّ رَّسُّولِ إَّلل َّهِ إ َّ ن َّسِي ث َّ إِعْي ِكَّاق َّك َّ ق َّق َّالَّ لَّهُّ  «
عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ي ُّحَّذِّب ُّ ع ن ْ ج َّذِّى  رَّسُّولِ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال ن  َّمَّا 
عَّن َّذَّ إَّلل َّهَّ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ ي ِسْعَّه َّ إ لأَّف ِ سَّن َّه ٍ صَّان ِماً ن َّهَّارَّةُّ ق َّان ِماً لَّي ْلَّه
hadiths.en:
It is narrated by Al-Hasan ibn Al-Jahm, from Abu Al-Hasan {a.s}, who was asked 
about a person performing i'tikaf engaging in relations with their spouse.   
Imam {a.s} said: "They must not approach their wife, neither by night nor by day, while 
they are in i'tikaf."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 179 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 11, p. 495 • Wasa’il 
Al-Shi’a, Vol. 10, p. 545 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 61, H. 2107

hadiths.chapter: 61. CHAPTER ON I'TIKAF (SPIRITUAL RETREAT)
hadiths.ar: كِت َاب ُ ألْحَج ِّ وَف َص َاب ِلِةِ
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Maymun ibn Mihran, who said:   
"I was sitting with Imam Al -Hasan ibn Ali {a.s} when a man came to Imam {a.s} and said, 
'O son of the Messenger of Allah {SWT}, so-and-so has a debt against me, and he intends 
to imprison me.'  
Imam Al-Hasan {a.s} said: 'By Allah {SWT}, I have no money to repay your debt.'  
The man then said: 'Could you speak to him on my behalf?'   
Imam Al-Hasan {a.s} put on his sandals.  
I said to Imam {a.s}: 'O son of the Messenger of Allah {SWT}, have you forgotten your i'tikaf?'  
Imam {a.s} replied: 'I have not forgotten. But I heard my father {a.s} narrating from my 
grandfather, the Messenger of Allah {SWT} (peace and blessings be upon him and his 
family), who said: “Whoever strives to fulfill the need of his Muslim brother, it is as if 
he worshipped Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic, for nine thousand years, fasting 
during the day and standing in prayer at night.”'   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Adab ad-Diniyah, Part 1, p. 152 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 5, p. 
668 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 10, p. 550 
BOOK OF PILGRIMAGE AND ITS VIRTUES 258 
BOOK OF PILGRIMAGE AND ITS VIRTUES

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 61, H. 2108

hadiths.chapter: 62. CHAPTER ON THE REASONS BEHIND HAJJ
hadiths.ar:
2110  -  وَّ ق َّذْ رُّوِى َّ : إ َّن  َّه ن َّي ْث ِ إَّلْمَّعْمُّورِ وَّ هُّوَّ مُّزَّن  َّغ  وَّ 
صَّارَّ إَّلْن َّي ْث ُّ إَّلْمَّعْمُّورُّ مُّزَّن  َّعاً لِأ َّن  َّهُّ ن ِحِذ َّإءِ إَّلْعَّزْس ِ وَّ هُّوَّ مُّزَّن  َّ غ  وَّ صَّارَّ إَّلْعَّزْس ُّ مُّزَّن  َّعاً لِأ َّن  َّ إَّلْكَّلِمَّاب ِ إَّل َّي ِى  ن ُّن ِى َّ عَّلَّي ْهَّا إَّلْأ ِسْلاَّمُّ 
إ َّرْن َّغ  وَّ هِى َّ: سُّن ْحَّان َّ إَّلل َّهِ وَّ إَّلْحَّمْذُّ لِل َّهِ وَّ لأَّ إ ِلَّهَّ إ ِلأ َّ إَّلل َّهُّ وَّ إَّلل َّهُّ إ َّكْن َّز
hadiths.en:
The Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said:   
"The Ka'bah was named 'Ka'bah' because it is the center of the world."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 62, H. 2109

hadiths.chapter: 62. CHAPTER ON THE REASONS BEHIND HAJJ
hadiths.en:
It is narrated: "The Ka'bah was named 'Ka'bah' because it is cubical in shape. 
It became cubical because it is directly beneath the Bayt al -Ma‘mur, which is also 
cubical.  
The Bayt al-Ma‘mur became cubical because it is directly beneath the Throne (‘Arsh), 
which is cubical as well.  
The Throne is cubical because the foundational words upon which Islam is built are 
four: Subhan Allah {SWT} (Glory be to Allah {SWT}), Alhamdulillah (Praise be to Allah {SWT}), 
La ilaha illa Allah {SWT} (There is no god but Allah {SWT}), and Allahu Akbar (Allah {SWT} is the 
Greatest)."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 62, H. 2110
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE REASONS BEHIND HAJJ
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON THE REASONS BEHIND HAJJ

hadiths.chapter: 62. CHAPTER ON THE REASONS BEHIND HAJJ
hadiths.en:
The House of Allah {SWT}, the Sacred (Bayt Allah {SWT} al-Haram), was so named 
because it was made forbidden (haram) for the polytheists to enter it.   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 62, H. 2111

reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON THE REASONS BEHIND HAJJ
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٢، ح ٢١١١

hadiths.chapter: 62. CHAPTER ON THE REASONS BEHIND HAJJ
hadiths.en:
The House (Ka'bah) was named al-Bayt al-Atiq (The Ancient House) because 
it was saved (freed) from being submerged in the flood.   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 62, H. 2112

hadiths.chapter: 62. CHAPTER ON THE REASONS BEHIND HAJJ
hadiths.ar:
2114  -  وَّ وُّص ِغَّ إَّلْن َّي ْث ُّ ق ِى  وَّسَّظِ إَّلْأ َّرْض ِ لِأ َّن  َّهُّ إَّلْمَّوْص ِغُّ إَّل َّذ ِى  مِن ْ ي َّحْي ِهِ ذُّجِي َّث ِ إَّلْأ َّرْض ُّ وَّ لِي َّكُّون َّ إَّلْ ق َّزْض ُّ لِأ َّهْلِ 
إَّلْمَّس ْزِق ِ وَّ إَّلْمَّع ْزِب ِ ق ِى  ذ َّلِك َّ سَّوَّإءً    
وإ ن ما ي ق ن ل إلحح ز وي سي لم لي و ذى  إ لى إلله عز  وج ل إلعهذ إلذ ى  إ ج ذ  علي هم ق ى  إلمي ي اق . وإ ن ما وص غ إلله عز  وج ل إلحح ز ق ى   
إلزكن  إلذ ى  هو ق ي ه ولم ي ص عه ق ى  ع ي زة لأ ن ه ي ن ارك  وي عالى جي ن  إ ج ذ  إلمي ي اق  إ ج ذ ة ق ى  ذ لك  إلمكان . وج زب  إلسن ه  ن الي كن ي ز  
وإسي ق ن ال إلزكن  إلذ ى  ق ي ه إلحح ز من  إلصق  ا، لأ ن ه لما ن ط ز إ ذم علي ه إلسلام من  إلصق ا وق ذ وص غ إلحح ز ق ى  إلزكن  كن ز إلله عز   
وج ل وهلله ومح ذة. وإ ن ما ج عل إلمي ي اق  ق ى  إلحح ز لأن  إلله عز  وج ل لما إ ج ذ  إلمي ي اق  له ن الزن ون ي ه  ولمحمذ صلى إلله علي ه 
وإ له ن الن ن وة  ولعلى  علي ه إلسلام ن الوصي ه  إصطكث  ق زإن ص إلملان كه  وإ ول من  إ س زع إ لى إلأق زإر ن ذ لك  إلحح ز ق لذ لك  إج ي ارة 
إلله عز  وج ل وإ لق مه إلمي ي اق  وهو ي ح ى  ي وم إلق ي امه  وله لسان  ن اطق  وعي ن  ن اط زة  ي س هذ لكل من  وإق اة إ لى ذ لك  إلمكان  وجق ظ  
إلمي ي اق . وإ ن ما إج زح  إلحح ز من  إلح ن ه  لي ذ كز إ ذم علي ه إلسلام ما ن سى  من  إلعهذ وإلمي ي اق . وصار إلحزم مق ذإر ما هو ل م ي كن   
إ ق ل ولأ إ كي ز لأن  إلله ي ن ارك  وي عالى إ هن ظ على إ ذم علي ه إلسلام ي اق وي ه  جمزإء ق وص عها ق ى  موص غ إلن ي ث  ق كان  ي طوف  ن ها  
إ ذم علي ه إلسلام وكان  ص وو ها ي ن لغ  موص غ إلأعلام ق علمث  إلأعلام على ص ون ها ق ح عله إلله عز  وج ل جزما. وإ ن ما ي سي لم  
إلحح ز لأن  موإي ي ق  إلح لان ق  ق ي ه، وكان  إ س  ذ ن ي اص ا من  إللن ن  ق اسوذ من  ج طاي ا ن ن ى  إ ذم، ولولأ ما مسه من  إ رج اس إلح اهلي ه   
ما مسه ذ و عاهه  إ لأ ن زء.
hadiths.en:
It is narrated:  
"It was named al -Atiq (the Ancient) because it is a house independent of people and 
has never been owned by anyone."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 62, H. 2113

hadiths.chapter: 62. CHAPTER ON THE REASONS BEHIND HAJJ
hadiths.ar:
2115  - وَّ سُّمِّى َّ إَّلْحَّطِي مُّ جَّطِي ماً لِأ َّن  َّ إَّلن  َّاسَّ ي َّحْطِمُّ ن َّعْص ُّهُّمْ ن َّعْص اً هُّن َّالِك  
وصار إلن اس ي سي لمون  إلحح ز وإلزكن  إلي مان ى  ولأ ي سي لمون  إلزكن ي ن  إلأ ج زي ن  لأن  إلحح ز إلأ سوذ وإلزكن  إلي مان ى  عن  ي مي ن   
إلعزس ، وإ ن ما إ مز إلله عز  وج ل إ ن  ي سي لم ما عن  ي مي ن  عزس ه.
hadiths.en:
The House (Ka'bah) was placed in the center of the Earth because it is the 
spot from beneath which the Earth was spread out, and so that its obligation could be 
equal for the people of both the East and the West.  
[AL SADUQ] 
The Black Stone (Hajar al -Aswad) is kissed and also touched to fulfill the covenant 
taken with Allah {SWT}, the Almighty, in the primordial covenant.   
Allah {SWT}, the Blessed and Exalted, placed the Black Stone in its current corner 
because He took the covenant from humanity in that specific location.   
The tradition of glorifying Allah {SWT} through takbeer (saying "Allahu Akbar") and facing 
the corner containing the Black Stone from the hill of Safa began because when Adam 
{a.s} looked from Safa and saw the Black Stone placed in the corner of the Ka'bah, he 
glorified and magnified Allah {SWT} with takbeer, tahleel (saying "La ilaha illa Allah {SWT}"), 
and tamjeed (praising).  
The covenant was placed in the Black Stone because when Allah {SWT}, the Exalted, took 
the covenant affirming His Lord {AZJ}ship, the Prophethood of Muhammad (peace and 
blessings be upon him and his family), and the leadership ( wasiyyah) of Ali {a.s}, the 
angels trembled in awe. The first to hasten to acknowledge and affirm this was the 
Black Stone. Thus, Allah {SWT} chose it, entrusted it with the covenant, and placed it as 
a witness. On the Day of Judgment, it will have a tongue that speaks and eyes that see, 
testifying on behalf of all who honored the covenant at that sacred place.   
The Black Stone was brought down from Paradise to remind Adam {a.s} of the covenant 
and pledge he had forgotten.  
The boundaries of the sanctuary (haram) are as they are because Allah {SWT} sent down 
a red ruby for Adam {a.s}, which was placed where the Ka'bah now stands. Adam would 
circumambulate it, and its light extended to the marked boundaries. The landmarks 
were established based on this illumination, designating the extent of the sanctuary.   
The Black Stone is touched because it holds the pledges of all creation. It was 
originally brighter than milk but became blackened due to the sins of humanity. Were 
it not for the impurities of ignorance that touched it, no afflicted person would touch it 
without being healed.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 62, H. 2114

hadiths.chapter: 62. CHAPTER ON THE REASONS BEHIND HAJJ
hadiths.ar:
2116 - وَّ إ ِن  َّمَّا صَّارَّ مَّق َّامُّ إ ِن ْزَّإهِي مَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ْ ي َّسَّارِةِ لِأ َّن  َّ لِأ ِن ْزَّإهِي مَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ مَّق َّام اً ق ِى  إَّلْق ِي َّامَّه ِ وَّ لِمُّحَّم َّذٍ  
صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ مَّق َّاماً ق َّمَّق َّامُّ مُّحَّم َّذٍ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ  لِهِ عَّن ْ ي َّمِي ن ِ عَّزْس ِ رَّن ِّن َّا عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ وَّ مَّق َّامُّ إ ِن ْزَّإهِي مَّ 
عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ْ س ِمَّالِ عَّزْس ِهِ ، ق َّمَّق َّامُّ إ ِن ْزَّإهِي مَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق ِى  مَّق َّامِهِ ي َّوْمَّ إَّلْق ِي َّامَّه ِ وَّ  عَّزْس ُّ رَّن ِّن َّا ي َّن َّارَّك َّ وَّ ي َّعَّالَّى  
مُّق ْن ِل  ع َّي ْزُّ مُّ ذْن ِزٍ وَّ صَّارَّ إَّلز ُّكْن ُّ إَّلس  َّامِى  ُّ مُّي َّحَّزِّكاً ق ِى  إَّلس ِّي َّاءِ وَّ إَّلص َّي ْف ِ وَّ إَّلل َّي ْلِ وَّ إَّلن  َّهَّارِ لِأ َّن  َّ إَّلزِّ ي حَّ مَّسْح ُّون َّه   ي َّحْي َّهُّ وَّ 
إ ِن  َّمَّا صَّارَّ إَّلْن َّي ْث ُّ مُّزْي َّق ِعاً ي ُّصْعَّذُّ إ ِلَّي ْهِ ن ِالذ َّرَّح ِ لِأ َّن  َّهُّ لَّم َّا هَّذَّمَّ إَّلْحَّح  َّا ح ُّ إَّلْكَّعْن َّه َّ ق َّز َّق َّ إَّلن  َّاسُّ ي ُّزَّإن َّهَّا، ق َّلَّم َّا إ َّرَّإذُّوإ إ َّن ْ ي َّن ْن ُّوهَّا  
ج َّزَّج َّث ْ عَّلَّي ْهِمْ جَّي  َّه   ق َّمَّن َّعَّث ِ إَّلن  َّاسَّ إَّلْن ِن َّاءَّ ق َّا ُّي ِى َّ إَّلْحَّح  َّاح ُّ ق َّا ُّج ْن ِزَّ ق َّسَّا َّلَّ إَّلْحَّح  َّاح ُّ عَّلِى  َّ ن ْ ن َّ إَّلْحُّسَّي ْن ِ عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ 
عَّن ْ ذ َّلِ ك َّ ق َّق َّالَّ لَّه جِي طَّان ُّهُّ إ َّمَّزَّ ن ِالي  ُّزَّإب ِ  
ق َّا ُّلْق ِى َّ ق ِى  ج َّوْق ِهِ ق َّلِذ َّلِك َّ صَّارَّ إَّلْن َّي ْث ُّ مُّزْي َّق ِعاً ي ُّصْعَّذُّ إ ِلَّي ْهِ ن ِالذ َّرَّح ِ وَّ صَّا رَّ إَّلن  َّاسُّ ي َّطُّوق ُّون َّ جَّوْلَّ إَّلْحِح ْزِ وَّ لأَّ ي َّطُّوق ُّون َّ 
ق ِي هِ لِأ َّن  َّ إ ُّم َّ إ ِسْمَّاعِي لَّ ذُّق ِن َّث ْ ق ِى  إَّلْحِح ْزِ ق َّق ِي هِ ق َّن ْزُّهَّا ق َّطِي ف َّ كَّذ َّلِك َّ كَّي ْلاَّ ي ُّوطَّا َّ ق َّن ْزُّهَّا
hadiths.en:
The area known as al-Hatim was named as such because people crowd and 
jostle each other there, "crushing" (yahtim) one another in that space.  
[AL SADUQ] 
As for why people touch ( istilam) the Black Stone and the Yemeni Corner while not 
touching the other two corners of the Ka'bah, it is because the Black Stone and the 
Yemeni Corner are aligned with the right side of the Divine Throne (al-‘Arsh). Allah {SWT}, 
the Exalted, specifically commanded the touching of what is on the right side of His 
Throne.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 62, H. 2115

hadiths.chapter: 62. CHAPTER ON THE REASONS BEHIND HAJJ
hadiths.ar:
2117  - وَّ رُّوِى    
وما ق ى  إلحح ز س ى  من  إلن ي ث  ولأ ق لامه  ط ق ز.
hadiths.en:
The station of Ibrahim (Maqam Ibrahim) is situated to the left because 
Ibrahim {a.s} has a position on the Day of Judgment, as does Muhammad {saws} (peace be 
upon him and his family). The position of Muhammad {saws} is to the right of the Divine 
Throne, while the position of Ibrahim {a.s} is to its left. This earthly location of the Maqam 
Ibrahim corresponds to Ibrahim's position in the Hereafter, while the Throne of Allah 
{SWT} (glorified and exalted) is ever-facing and not turned away.  
The Syrian Corner of the Ka'bah moves during the winter and summer, day and night, 
due to the winds trapped beneath it. The Ka'bah is elevated, and people ascend to it 
via steps because when Al -Hajjaj destroyed the Ka'bah, the people dispersed its soil. 
When they sought to rebuild it, a serpent emerged and prevented them from 
continuing. When Al -Hajjaj was informed, he consulted Ali ibn Al -Husayn {a.s}, who 
instructed that everyone return the soil they had taken. After the walls were raised, the 
remaining s oil was placed inside the Ka'bah, leading to its elevated state, requiring 
steps for access.  
As for why people circumambulate the Hijr Ismail without entering it, it is because the 
grave of Ismail’s mother (peace be upon her) is located within the Hijr. 
Circumambulating around it was established so that her grave would not be stepped 
upon.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 62, H. 2116
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE REASONS BEHIND HAJJ
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON THE REASONS BEHIND HAJJ

hadiths.chapter: 62. CHAPTER ON THE REASONS BEHIND HAJJ
hadiths.ar: 2118  - وَّ سُّمِّي َّث ْ ن َّك َّه َّ لِأ َّن  َّ إَّلن  َّاسَّ ي َّن ُّك  ُّ ن َّعْص ُّهُّمْ ن َّعْص اً ق ِي هَّا ن ِالْأ َّي ْذِى
hadiths.en:
It has been narrated that within the Hijr Ismail lie the graves of Prophets {a.s}.   
[AL SADUQ] 
However, nothing from the House (Ka'bah) itself, not even a clipping of a fingernail, is 
present in the Hijr.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 62, H. 2117
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE REASONS BEHIND HAJJ
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON THE REASONS BEHIND HAJJ
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٢، ح ٢١١٧

hadiths.chapter: 62. CHAPTER ON THE REASONS BEHIND HAJJ
hadiths.ar:
2119  - وَّ رُّوِى    
ون كه  هو موص غ إلن ي ث  وإلق زي ه  مكه . وإ ن ما لأ ي سي حث  إلهذى  إ لى إلكعن ه  لأ ن ه ي صي ز إ لى إلحح ن ه  ذون  إلمساكي ن  وإلكعن ه  لأ  
ي ا كل ولأ ي س زب  وما ج عل هذي ا لها ق هو لز وإرها وروى  إ ن ه ي ن اذى على إلحح ز: إ لأ من  إن ق طعث  ن ه إلن ق ق ه  ق لي حص ز ق ي ذق غ إ لي ه.
hadiths.en:
It is named Bakkah because people crowd against one another there, 
pressing with their hands.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 62, H. 2118
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE REASONS BEHIND HAJJ
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON THE REASONS BEHIND HAJJ
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٢، ح ٢١١٨

hadiths.chapter: 62. CHAPTER ON THE REASONS BEHIND HAJJ
hadiths.ar:
وَّ رُّوِى  
2120  - وَّ إ ِن  َّمَّا هَّذَّمَّث ْ ق ُّزَّي ْس   إَّلْكَّعْن َّه َّ لِأ َّن  َّ إَّلس َّي ْلَّ كَّان َّ ي َّا ْي ِي هِمْ مِن ْ إ َّعْلَّى مَّك َّه َّ ق َّي َّذْج ُّلُّهَّا ق َّان ْصَّذَّع ث ْ
hadiths.en:
It is narrated that it was named Bakkah because of the weeping of people 
around and within it. 
[AL SADUQ] 
Bakkah refers specifically to the area around the House (Ka'bah), while the 
surrounding settlement is called Makkah.  
Hadiyah (offering) is not recommended to be sent directly to the Ka'bah because it 
would end up with the custodians of the Ka'bah rather than reaching the needy. The 
Ka'bah does not eat or drink; any offering made for it is intended for its visitors.   
It is also narrated that there is an announcement at the stone: "Whoever has run out 
of provisions should come forward, and it will be provided to them."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾ 
It is narrated: "There is an announcement made at the Hajar (stone): 'Whoever has run 
out of provisions should come forward, and it will be provided to them.'"   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 13, p. 253 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 62, H. 2119
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE REASONS BEHIND HAJJ
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON THE REASONS BEHIND HAJJ
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٢، ح ٢١١٩

hadiths.chapter: 62. CHAPTER ON THE REASONS BEHIND HAJJ
hadiths.ar:
2121  - وَّ سُّن ِلَّ إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : عَّن ْ ق َّوْلِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل ۝ سَّوٰإءً إَّلْعٰاكِف ُّ ق ِي هِ وَّ إَّلْن ٰاذِ ۝» 
ق َّق َّال ورِهِمْ ق ِى  سَّاجَّه ِ إَّلذ َّإرِ جَّي  َّى  
ي َّق ْص ُّوإ مَّن َّاسِكَّهُّمْ ق َّا ِن  َّ إ َّو َّلَّ مَّن ْ ج َّعَّلَّ لِذُّورِ مَّك َّه َّ إ َّن ْوَّإن اً مُّعَّاوِي َّه    
وي كزة إلمق ام ن مكه  لأن  رسول إلله صلى إلله علي ه وإ له إج زح  عن ها، وإلمق ي م ن ها ي ق سو ق لن ه جي ى ي ا ي ى  ق ي ها ما ي ا ي ى  ق ى  
ع ي زها. ولم  ي عذ ب  ماء ر مز م لأ ن ها ن ع ث  على إلمي اة ق ا ج زى إلله عز  وج ل إ لي ها عي ن ا من  صن ز. وإ ن ما صار ماء ر مز م ي عذ ب  ق ى   
وق ث  ذون  وق ث  لأ ن ه ي ح زى  إ لي ها عي ن  من  ي حث  إلحح ز ق ا ذ إ ع لن ث  ماء إلعي ن  عذ ب  ماء ر مز م. وإ ن ما سمى  إلصق ا صق ا لأن  
إلمصطق ى إ ذم علي ه إلسلام هن ظ علي ه ق ق طغ للح ن ل إسم من  إسم   إ ذم علي ه إلسلام لق ول إلله عز  وج ل: " ۝  إ ن  إلله إصطق ى 
إ ذم ون وجا ۝" وهن طث  جوإ على إلمزوة  ق سمي ث  إلمزوة  لأ ن  إلمزإ ة  هن طث  علي ه ق ق طغ للح ن ل إسم من  إسم إلمزإ ة .
hadiths.en:
And it is narrated: 
"Indeed, Quraysh demolished the Kaaba because floods would come from the higher 
regions of Mecca, entering it and causing it to crack."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Ilal al-Sharai’, Vol. 2, p. 449 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 96, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 62, H. 2120
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE REASONS BEHIND HAJJ
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON THE REASONS BEHIND HAJJ
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٢، ح ٢١٢٠

hadiths.chapter: 62. CHAPTER ON THE REASONS BEHIND HAJJ
hadiths.en:
And it is narrated:  
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} was asked about the saying of Allah {SWT}, the 
Mighty and Glorious: “...wherein the residents and the visitors are equal...”  (Surah Al-
Hajj 22:25).  
Imam {a.s} replied: "It was not appropriate to construct doors on the houses of Mecca 
because the pilgrims had the right to stay with the residents in their courtyards until 
they completed their rituals. The first to place doors on the houses of Mecca was 
Muawiyah."  
[AL SADUQ] 
It is discouraged to reside permanently in Mecca because the Messenger of Allah {SWT}, 
peace and blessings be upon him and his family, was driven out from it. Living there 
can harden one's heart to the extent that a person may commit deeds in Mecca similar 
to what they would commit elsewhere.   
The water of Zamzam was not originally salty, but it acted arrogantly toward other 
waters. Therefore, Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Glorious, caused a spring of patience to 
flow into it.  Zamzam's water becomes salty at certain times because a spring flows 
into it from beneath the stone. When the spring's water overpowers Zamzam's natural 
flow, its taste changes to salty.  
The name "Safa" comes from the "chosen one" (Mustafa), as Prophet Adam {a.s} 
descended upon it. The mountain was named after Adam because of Allah’s {SWT} 
words: “Indeed, Allah {SWT} chose Adam and Noah...” (Surah Aal-E-Imran 3:33).  
Hawa (Eve) descended upon "Marwah," and the mountain was named after her 
because it took part of the woman's (mar'ah) name.   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Reasons for the Shariah, Vol. 2, p. 396 • Tafsir al-
Burhan, vol. 3, p. 868 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 96, p. 81 • Tafsir Nur Al-Thaqalayn, Part 3, p. 481 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 62, H. 2121
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE REASONS BEHIND HAJJ
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON THE REASONS BEHIND HAJJ
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٦٢، ح ٢١٢١

hadiths.chapter: 62. CHAPTER ON THE REASONS BEHIND HAJJ
hadiths.ar:
2123  -  وَّ "إ ِن  َّ إلل َّهَّ ي َّن َّارَّك َّ وَّي َّعَّالَّى ج َّعَّلَّ إلْكَّعْن َّه َّ ق ِن ْلَّه ً لِأ َّهْلِ إلْمَّسْح ِذِ، وَّج َّعَّلَّ إلْمَّسْح ِذَّ ق ِن ْلَّه ً لِأ َّهْ لِ إلْحَّزَّمِ، وَّج َّعَّلَّ إلْحَّزَّمَّ 
ق ِن ْلَّه ً لِأ َّهْلِ إلذ ُّن ْي َّا.  وَّإ ِن  َّمَّا ج ُّعِلَّث ِ إلي  َّلْن ِي َّه ُّ لِأ َّن  َّ إلل َّهَّ عَّز  َّ وَّج َّل َّ لَّم َّا ق َّالَّ لِأ ِن ْزَّإهِي مَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إلس َّلَّامُّ" ۝  وَّإ َّذ ِّن ْ ق ِى  إلن  َّاسِ ن ِالْحَّح ِّ 
ي َّا ْي ُّوك َّ رِج َّالًأ ۝ "ق َّن َّاذَّى ق َّا ُّج ِي ث َّ مِن ْ كُّلِّ ق َّح ٍّ ي ُّلَّن  ُّون َّ".
hadiths.en:
"The mosque was made sacred because of the Kaaba, the sanctuary was 
made sacred because of the mosque, and Ihram became obligatory because of the 
sanctuary."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al Mahasin, Part 2, p. 330 • Ilal al-Sharai’, Vol. 2, p. 415 
• Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 12, p. 314 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 96, p. 43 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 96, p. 134 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 62, H. 2122
